facts.text: Patient presents for a routine physical/health check ('tune-up') at his brother's urging, having not had a physical in years
facts.transcript_quote: you're fifty-four, you haven't had a proper physical in years, go before something finds you first

facts.text: Dull headaches at the back of the head on some mornings for a few months, fading by lunchtime
facts.transcript_quote: I get these dull headaches at the back of my head some mornings... A few months? They fade by lunchtime.

facts.text: Bleeding gums when brushing
facts.transcript_quote: my gums bleed when I brush... Just a pink sink, most days.

facts.text: Depression screen (PHQ-2) score of 2 — low, no treatment indicated
facts.transcript_quote: Those answers put you at a two on the depression screen... both low, not in a range where I'd push treatment

facts.text: Anxiety screen (GAD-7) score of 3 — low, no treatment indicated
facts.transcript_quote: a three on the anxiety scale — both low

facts.text: Alcohol use: 1-2 beers on 2-3 evenings per week; AUDIT-C score 3, below concern threshold for men
facts.transcript_quote: Two or three evenings a week I'll have a beer or two... That scores a three on the alcohol screen — below the level we worry about for men

facts.text: Never smoked tobacco
facts.transcript_quote: Never. I watched my uncle go from cigarettes.

facts.text: Denies illicit drug use (marijuana, non-prescribed pills)
facts.transcript_quote: No, nothing.

facts.text: Blood pressure elevated at 141/100 mmHg, confirmed on repeat
facts.transcript_quote: Your blood pressure is 141 over 100 — and I rechecked it myself after you'd been sitting a while, same story.

facts.text: Heart rate 100 /min
facts.transcript_quote: Heart rate's 100 today too.

facts.text: Body weight 93.9 kg with BMI 30.21 kg/m2, over the obesity line
facts.transcript_quote: Your weight is 93.9 kilos, BMI 30.21, so over the obesity line.

facts.text: Hemoglobin A1c 6.28% consistent with prediabetes
facts.transcript_quote: Your A1c came back at 6.28 percent — that's prediabetes, drifting the wrong direction.

facts.text: Blood glucose 91 mg/dL, normal
facts.transcript_quote: Glucose today was 91, which is fine day-to-day.

facts.text: Creatinine 1.12 mg/dL and BUN 17.36 mg/dL, normal kidney function
facts.transcript_quote: Kidneys look good — creatinine 1.12, BUN 17.36.

facts.text: Sodium 139 mmol/L
facts.transcript_quote: Sodium 139, potassium 3.97.

facts.text: Potassium 3.97 mmol/L
facts.transcript_quote: Sodium 139, potassium 3.97.

facts.text: Diagnosis of essential hypertension
facts.transcript_quote: this is real — essential hypertension, high blood pressure as its own condition

facts.text: Diagnosis of metabolic syndrome (hypertension, prediabetes, obesity cluster)
facts.transcript_quote: That cluster has a name — metabolic syndrome.

facts.text: Started hydrochlorothiazide 25 mg oral tablet, one tablet every morning with breakfast
facts.transcript_quote: hydrochlorothiazide, 25 milligrams, one tablet every morning. It's a water pill

facts.text: Counseled that hydrochlorothiazide can cause lightheadedness on standing and muscle cramps; instructed to call if these occur, with bloodwork recheck planned for potassium/sodium
facts.transcript_quote: If you feel lightheaded when you stand, or you get muscle cramps, call us — it can nudge your potassium and sodium, so we'll recheck bloodwork after you've been on it a while.

facts.text: Vitamin B12 injectable continued unchanged for anemia
facts.transcript_quote: the B12 injections continue unchanged, that's for your anemia and it's doing its job

facts.text: Medication reconciliation completed: current meds are vitamin B12 injectable and hydrochlorothiazide 25 mg each morning; no other supplements/herbals
facts.transcript_quote: your list is the vitamin B12 injectable, and now hydrochlorothiazide 25 milligrams each morning... Nothing else.

facts.text: Counseled on low-salt dietary changes (reduce deli meat, sausage, canned soups, chips; cook more at home)
facts.transcript_quote: salt is the big lever. Deli meat, sausage, canned soups, chips... Aim for cooking more of what you eat

facts.text: Counseled to walk 30 minutes most days
facts.transcript_quote: walking: thirty minutes, most days. Take the kids' dog.

facts.text: Enrolled in hypertension education program
facts.transcript_quote: I'll enroll you in our hypertension education program

facts.text: Gingivitis identified on exam — puffy gum line, bleeds with light contact; reversible stage of gum disease
facts.transcript_quote: the gum line is puffy and bleeds with light contact. That's gingivitis.

facts.text: Dental referral placed for cleaning and exam; patient not seen dentist in 4-5 years
facts.transcript_quote: I'm sending a dental referral today — cleaning and an exam.

facts.text: Counseled on oral hygiene: brush twice daily with soft brush, floss even when bleeding; bleeding fades within a couple weeks
facts.transcript_quote: Brush twice daily, soft brush, and floss even when it bleeds; the bleeding fades as the gums get healthier, usually within a couple of weeks.

facts.text: Influenza vaccine (split virus, trivalent, preservative-free) administered in left arm
facts.transcript_quote: flu vaccine — left arm, I heard? Small pinch... done.

facts.text: Follow-up scheduled in about one month for blood pressure recheck; advised to keep a home BP log (morning, sitting, feet on floor)
facts.transcript_quote: I want to see you back in about a month for a blood pressure recheck. If you can get hold of a home cuff, even better

facts.text: Counseled that morning headaches likely related to hypertension and expected to improve as BP is controlled; further workup if they persist
facts.transcript_quote: morning headaches and high pressure often travel together. My honest expectation is they fade as the numbers come down.

facts.text: SDOH: Patient stopped working last year after being primary caregiver for his mother until her death last fall; not currently seeking work
facts.transcript_quote: I stopped working last year and I'm not looking... I was her person, day and night, until she passed last fall.

facts.text: SDOH: Very low income (~$4,829/year) from odd jobs; lives with sister's family (5 people in household); has housing and feels safe; Medicaid insurance
facts.transcript_quote: my own income this year is next to nothing — a few thousand dollars, odd jobs. My sister feeds me

facts.text: SDOH: Family unable to obtain needed medicine or health care (medical/dental/mental health/vision) in past year per PRAPARE
Grief following mother's death with variable mood; patient does not consider himself depressed; counseled that worsening grief is a conversation to revisit
facts.transcript_quote: Grief keeps its own schedule. If it gets heavier, that's a conversation, not a confession.

facts.text: Notes fogginess and heavy legs when B12 injections are skipped
facts.transcript_quote: I do notice when I skip — I get foggy and my legs feel like sandbags.

facts.text: Pain severity score 1/10 reported
Respiratory rate 13 /min
Calcium 9.7 mg/dL, chloride 106.14 mmol/L, CO2 22.84 mmol/L (basic metabolic panel results)
Body height 176.3 cm

presence.rationale: HCTZ 25 mg oral each morning started.

presence.note_evidence: Continue vitamin B12 5 mg/mL injectable solution on the established schedule

presence.note_evidence: dietary emphasis on reduced processed/salty foods
regular walking
presence.rationale: Walking counseled but 30 minutes/most days not specified.

presence.note_evidence: Enrolled in lifestyle education regarding hypertension
erythematous, edematous gingival margins with contact bleeding, consistent with gingivitis
presence.rationale: Gingivitis with puffy bleeding gum margins documented.

presence.note_evidence: Patient referral for dental care placed (cleaning and examination)
presence.rationale: Dental referral documented; 'not seen dentist 4-5 years' not mentioned.

presence.note_evidence: soft brush twice daily, daily flossing
presence.rationale: Oral hygiene counseling documented; minor extra details omitted.

presence.note_evidence: Influenza vaccine (split virus, trivalent, preservative-free) administered
presence.rationale: Vaccine administered but injection site (left arm) not documented.

presence.note_evidence: Recheck BP in approximately 1 month ... home BP log
presence.rationale: One-month BP follow-up and home BP log documented.

presence.note_evidence: confirmed on repeat measurement, with associated morning occipital headaches
presence.rationale: Headache-hypertension link noted but counseling on improvement/further workup not documented.

presence.note_evidence: left full-time work last year after a prolonged period caring for his mother, who died last fall; he is not currently seeking work
presence.rationale: Caregiving, bereavement, and not seeking work documented.

presence.note_evidence: minimal personal income, living in a five-person household with his sister's family ... reported personal annual income $4,829
presence.rationale: Income and household documented but Medicaid insurance and 'odd jobs' not stated.
No PRAPARE finding of inability to obtain medicine/care documented.

presence.note_evidence: intermittent low mood and worry consistent with grief but denies persistent depression ... Supportive counseling
presence.rationale: Grief, variable mood, not depressed, and supportive counseling documented.

presence.note_evidence: reports fatigue and leg heaviness when injections are missed
presence.rationale: Fatigue/heavy legs when B12 missed documented.

presence.note_evidence: pain score 1/10
RR 13/min
calcium 9.7 mg/dL ... chloride 106.14 mmol/L, total CO2 22.84 mmol/L
height 176.3 cm
